title: git_with_timeout in worktree.rs reimplements process timeout; not shared with pdf reader
description:
## Severity
Minor

## Dimension
DRY / Decoupling

## Location
src/tools/worktree.rs:15-46 (git_with_timeout) vs src/tools/file/read.rs:142-173 (pdftotext invocation with no timeout)

## Violation
worktree.rs implements a polling-based subprocess timeout helper that spawns, polls try_wait every 100ms, kills+reaps on deadline. read.rs's pdftotext invocation (line 156: match cmd.output()) does NOT use any timeout, despite being subject to the same class of hang. The helper is hard-coded to git — it is not reusable. Two stovepiped subprocess strategies in the same subsystem.

## Mandated refactor
Lift the helper into src/tools/command.rs as pub fn run_with_timeout(program: &str, args: &[&str], timeout: Duration) -> Result<Output, CommandError>. Use it in BOTH worktree.rs (git) and read.rs (pdftotext, pdfinfo). The polling-with-sleep approach is also poor — replace with wait_timeout crate or tokio::process::Command + tokio::time::timeout when called from async contexts.

## Filed from QA review dispatch
